cost_of_revenue.calculation_note: Cost of revenue — Condensed Consolidated Statements of Income, page 5

operating_expenses.calculation_note: Sum of Research and development (15,144) + Marketing and sales (2,845) + General and administrative (3,512) = 21,501. Excludes Cost of revenue. Condensed Consolidated Statements of Income, page 5

cash_and_equivalents.calculation_note: Cash and cash equivalents — Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets, page 6

short_term_debt.calculation_note: No current portion of long-term debt or short-term borrowings identified as a separate line item on the Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets, page 6. Operating lease liabilities, current (2,113) excluded per rules.

long_term_debt.calculation_note: Long-term debt — Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets, page 6

net_debt.calculation_note: Short-term debt (0) + Long-term debt (28,834) - Cash & Cash Equivalents (10,187) = Net debt (18,647). Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets, page 6

capital_expenditure.calculation_note: Purchases of property and equipment — Condensed Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows, page 7

free_cash_flow.calculation_note: Free cash flow — Reconciliation of GAAP to Non-GAAP Results, page 9. Calculated as Net cash provided by operating activities (29,999) minus Purchases of property and equipment (18,829) minus Principal payments on finance leases (545).

shares_outstanding.calculation_note: Total shares outstanding as of period end date not explicitly stated as a single line item in the financial statements provided.

shares_weighted_average_diluted.calculation_note: Weighted-average shares used to compute earnings per share — Diluted — Condensed Consolidated Statements of Income, page 5

sections_found.summary: Meta reported Q3 2025 revenue of $51.24 billion, up 26% year-over-year, with operating income of $20.54 billion (40% margin). Reported net income dropped 83% to $2.71 billion due to a one-time non-cash tax charge of $15.93 billion; excluding this charge, adjusted net income would have been $18.64 billion.

Implementation of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act triggered a $15.93 billion non-cash income tax charge from a valuation allowance against U.S. federal deferred tax assets related to the Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax, inflating the effective tax rate to 87%. The company expects significant reductions in future U.S. federal cash tax payments as a result of the same legislation.
Family daily active people reached 3.54 billion in September 2025, up 8% year-over-year. Ad impressions grew 14% and average price per ad rose 10% year-over-year, driving the 26% revenue increase.

Capital expenditures including finance lease payments were $19.37 billion for Q3 2025, reflecting aggressive infrastructure investment. Operating cash flow was $30.0 billion, while free cash flow was $10.63 billion after deducting capex and lease payments.

The Family of Apps segment generated $50.77 billion in revenue and $24.97 billion in operating income for Q3 2025. Reality Labs contributed $470 million in revenue but posted an operating loss of $4.43 billion, consistent with the prior year loss of $4.43 billion.

Q4 2025 revenue is guided at $56-59 billion with a ~1% FX tailwind; full-year 2025 expenses are expected at $116-118 billion and capex at $70-72 billion. For 2026, management expects notably larger capex dollar growth and significantly faster expense growth driven by infrastructure and AI talent costs.

Meta faces potential significant negative impact on European revenue from EU regulatory actions on its Less Personalized Ads offering, possibly as early as Q4 2025. In the U.S., multiple youth-related trials scheduled for 2026 could result in material losses.

Meta repurchased $3.16 billion of Class A common stock and paid $1.33 billion in dividends and dividend equivalents in Q3 2025. Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities stood at $44.45 billion as of September 30, 2025.
